rationale: The API, UI, durable producers, consumers, and schema ship as one single-host revision. Independent runtime disablement would permit unsupported producer/consumer splits and cannot roll back committed schema or filesystem effects.
admittedBeforeRevisionChange: The running revision owns admitted work until its durable terminal or reconciliation state.
oldReaderBehavior: Unsupported for rows written by a newer schema; an old binary must not be restarted against an upgraded database.
newReaderBehavior: The new revision reads pre-upgrade rows and migrations backfill required projections before admission.
reEnableBehavior: Deploy a forward-fixed complete server revision; no partial UI-only re-enable path exists.
